summary:
Astrocytes do not form the main structure of the blood-brain barrier but contribute critically by stabilizing it via their end-feet surrounding cerebral capillaries. Their functional regulation is essential for preserving BBB integrity; thus, they are regarded as an active participant within the BBB system.  

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant facts related to the query about astrocyte involvement with the blood-brain barrier.
